refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Document operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task complexity, and any safety/policy deviations that affected throughput.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations with mechanical efficiency and cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target productivity.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits) without reducing productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall introduce latency and control fidelity such that remote operator productivity ≥ on-site operator productivity baseline.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-power hydraulic drivetrain with optimized bucket geometry and cycle automation to minimize per-task time; couple with real-time telemetry feedback to operator.
DP-B: Implement modular task execution framework (load, move, dump, return) with operator-selectable automation levels; measure and report cycle metrics continuously.
DP-C: Deploy integrated vehicle control system with predictive load estimation and adaptive engine throttle; pair with operator coaching interface to sustain target pace.

DP-D: Combine mechanical productivity (vehicle design) with operational productivity (operator training, task planning, safety constraint tuning) via closed-loop performance monitoring and adaptive task allocation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Combine mechanical productivity (vehicle design, hydraulic efficiency, cycle automation) with operational productivity (operator interface responsiveness, safety constraint tuning, real-time performance feedback) via closed-loop monitoring and adaptive task allocation across both on-site and remote operator modes.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical design (DP-D mechanical component) is independent of operator interface design (DP-D operational component); both feed into a common performance measurement system.
Safety constraint tuning (DP-D safety component) may couple with productivity if constraints are overly conservative; mitigation is explicit trade-off analysis during safety services design.
Teleoperation latency (DP-D remote mode) couples with operator control responsiveness; mitigation is latency budget allocation and operator training.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a system-level design that decouples mechanical and operational concerns via a measurement and feedback layer. This is appropriate for a high-level FR that spans multiple domains (mechanical, control, human factors, safety). Coupling is managed through explicit interface definitions (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) and performance feedback loops.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Operator training and task planning are within scope of NGHE solution delivery; if not, productivity target may be unachievable.
Assumption: Worksite conditions (soil, weather, task profile) are representative of the evaluation period; significant variation may invalidate productivity target.
Risk: Teleoperation latency may degrade remote operator productivity below on-site baseline; mitigation requires latency budget ≤ 200 ms and operator familiarization.
Risk: Safety constraint enforcement (geofencing, load limits) may reduce achievable productivity; mitigation is explicit trade-off analysis and constraint tuning during safety services design.
Risk: Operator fatigue and situational awareness degradation over extended shifts may reduce sustained productivity; mitigation is operator interface ergonomics and real-time fatigue monitoring.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but should be refined to explicitly separate mechanical productivity drivers (vehicle design, hydraulic efficiency, cycle automation) from operational productivity drivers (operator interface, safety constraint tuning, performance feedback). This separation will clarify ownership boundaries and enable independent optimization of each domain.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates vehicle mechanical capability, teleoperation system (if deployed), safety services, and operator interface to deliver measurable productivity. It owns the closed-loop performance monitoring, adaptive task allocation, and cross-system constraint tuning required to sustain target productivity across both on-site and remote operator modes.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time vehicle state (position, load, cycle time, fuel consumption, safety status) streamed to operator interface and performance monitoring system. Enables operator situational awareness and closed-loop productivity feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (move, load, dump, return) and task parameters (target location, load limit, cycle time target) received from operator interface. Enables adaptive task allocation and constraint enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts (geofence breach, load limit exceeded, operator fatigue detected, emergency stop) transmitted to operator and safety services. Enables real-time safety constraint enforcement and incident prevention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for teleoperation mode (remote operator commands and vehicle telemetry). Enables remote operation with bounded latency and reliability.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for productivity KPI delivery. It owns the integration logic, performance monitoring, and adaptive task allocation. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns mechanical productivity drivers (hydraulic efficiency, cycle automation, load capacity). However, productivity KPI also depends on operator interface, safety constraint tuning, and teleoperation latency, which are outside Vehicle scope. High diffusion risk if productivity ownership is assigned to Vehicle alone.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and latency management. However, productivity also depends on vehicle mechanical capability and safety constraint tuning, which are outside Teleoperation scope. Productivity KPI cannot be owned by Teleoperation alone.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (geofencing, load limits, speed limits). However, productivity KPI depends on vehicle capability and operator interface, which are outside Safety Services scope. Safety Services influences productivity but does not own it.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Productivity KPI (CR-NGHE-001) is a system-level acceptance criterion that depends on the integrated behavior of vehicle, teleoperation, safety services, and operator interface. No single subsystem module can own this KPI independently. NGHE Solution is the appropriate container because it orchestrates all subsystems and owns the closed-loop performance monitoring and adaptive task allocation required to sustain target productivity.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is treated as a passive integration layer without active performance monitoring and adaptive control, productivity KPI ownership becomes diffuse and accountability is unclear.
Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are optimized independently without coordination through NGHE Solution, local optimizations may conflict and reduce overall productivity.

Risk: If operator interface design (on-site or remote) is not tightly integrated with NGHE Solution performance monitoring, operator situational awareness and control responsiveness may degrade, reducing productivity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: NGHE Solution ownership is appropriate. However, the IM boundary should be refined to explicitly define the performance monitoring and adaptive task allocation subsystems that enable closed-loop productivity control. These subsystems should be designed as owned components within NGHE Solution, with clear interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (integrated mechanical and operational productivity with closed-loop monitoring) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (achieve productivity target). The DP separates mechanical drivers (vehicle design, hydraulic efficiency) from operational drivers (operator interface, safety constraint tuning) and couples them through a measurement and feedback layer. This is a sound design approach for a system-level productivity KPI.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is implemented as a thin integration layer without active performance monitoring, the closed-loop feedback mechanism (key to DP-D) may not be realized, and productivity KPI ownership becomes diffuse.
Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are developed independently without coordination through NGHE Solution, the adaptive task allocation mechanism (key to DP-D) may not be realized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate container for DP-D because it owns the integration logic, performance monitoring, and adaptive task allocation. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are subsystems that contribute to productivity but do not own the KPI independently. Clear interface definitions (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) enable subsystem independence while maintaining system-level coordination.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Productivity KPI verification is feasible via representative operations trials. Measurement requires instrumentation of vehicle (cycle time, load, fuel consumption) and operator interface (command latency, situational awareness metrics). Verification should be conducted across both on-site and remote operator modes to validate DP-D assumption that remote productivity ≥ on-site baseline.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Productivity KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution as the top-level system. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (mechanical efficiency), Teleoperation (latency and control fidelity), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement without productivity degradation) are defined in sub-FRs. Operator interface design (on-site or remote) is a critical component of NGHE Solution that must be explicitly designed and verified.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to explicitly define the performance monitoring and adaptive task allocation subsystems within NGHE Solution. These subsystems should have clear ownership and interfaces.
Define latency budget for teleoperation mode (e.g., ≤ 200 ms end-to-end) to ensure remote operator productivity ≥ on-site baseline.

Define safety constraint tuning process to ensure constraints are enforced without reducing productivity below target. This should be a collaborative process involving Safety Manager and Site Manager.

Define operator interface design requirements (on-site and remote) to ensure sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness. This should include ergonomics, display design, and control responsiveness metrics.
Define verification plan for productivity KPI, including representative operations trials, measurement methodology, and acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing mechanical productivity (vehicle design), operational productivity (operator interface), teleoperation latency and control fidelity, and safety constraint tuning. The refined FR and sub-FRs are well-defined and ready for the next decomposition level. DP-D is sound and appropriately assigned to NGHE Solution. However, the next level of decomposition is necessary to define concrete design parameters for each sub-FR and assign them to appropriate subsystem modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR-NGHE-001 has been decomposed into four sub-FRs that address distinct aspects of productivity delivery. Each sub-FR is ready for independent design and verification. The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate container for the top-level productivity KPI, but subsystem modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must be designed to support the sub-FRs. Decomposition should proceed to the next level to define concrete DPs and IMs for each sub-FR.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations with mechanical efficiency and cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time. [Owned by Vehicle module]

FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target productivity. [Owned by NGHE Solution operator interface subsystem]
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce operational constraints (speed limits, geofencing, load limits) without reducing productivity below target. [Owned by Safety Services module]

FR-NGHE-001.4: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall introduce latency and control fidelity such that remote operator productivity ≥ on-site operator productivity baseline. [Owned by Teleoperation module]

assumptions: Operator training and task planning are within scope of NGHE solution delivery.
Worksite conditions (soil, weather, task profile) are representative of the evaluation period.
Remote operator productivity can be made ≥ on-site baseline through latency management and operator training.
Safety constraint enforcement can be tuned to avoid reducing productivity below target.
Productivity measurement methodology (m³/h or ton/h) is well-defined and repeatable across evaluation period.
open_risks: Teleoperation latency may degrade remote operator productivity below on-site baseline; mitigation requires latency budget ≤ 200 ms and operator familiarization.
Safety constraint enforcement may reduce achievable productivity; mitigation is explicit trade-off analysis and constraint tuning.
Operator fatigue and situational awareness degradation may reduce sustained productivity; mitigation is operator interface ergonomics and real-time fatigue monitoring.
Worksite conditions may vary significantly from representative operations, invalidating productivity target.
Operator skill and experience level may significantly affect achievable productivity; baseline operator profile must be defined.
evidence_needed: Latency budget allocation for teleoperation mode (end-to-end latency ≤ 200 ms).
Safety constraint tuning process and trade-off analysis (safety vs. productivity).
Operator interface design requirements (on-site and remote) with ergonomics and control responsiveness metrics.
Verification plan for productivity KPI, including representative operations trials, measurement methodology, and acceptance criteria.
Baseline operator profile (skill level, experience, training) for productivity target definition.
Worksite condition profile (soil type, task complexity, weather) for representative operations trials.
